title: Sound Carrying from the Field Hospital
description: Standing at the abatis line, you can hear the field hospital clearly—a low, continuous groaning that carries across the empty earthworks. Mifflin points south: if you can hear it from here, the British pickets two hundred yards beyond the abatis may also be able to hear it when the wind is right. The sound does not simulate an occupied garrison; it simulates a hospital. The acoustic deception is partially compromised by the wounded men's involuntary noise.
